Technical Memo

XRD Batch Analysis — Decision Memo

Date: 2026-05-07  •  Analyst: Automated Pipeline · Sam Dhole  •  Samples: 8  •  Method: lmfit PseudoVoigt · Scherrer K=0.9 · RRUFF database · wavelength per file header (default Cu Kα λ=1.54056 Å)

Executive Summary

This portfolio demo turns repeat materials-characterization data into a decision-ready technical memo. The dominant reflection remains position-stable at 26.65° 2θ. FWHM broadens from 0.0794° to 0.2205°. Scherrer upper-bound crystallite size decreases from 102.8 nm to 37.0 nm. Mean fit quality R²=0.811; No samples flagged for review.

Process Signal

The dominant reflection remains position-stable at 26.65° 2θ. FWHM broadens from 0.0794° to 0.2205°. Scherrer upper-bound crystallite size decreases from 102.8 nm to 37.0 nm.

Signal 1

The dominant reflection remains position-stable at 26.65° 2θ.

Signal 2

FWHM broadens from 0.0794° to 0.2205°.

Signal 3

Scherrer upper-bound crystallite size decreases from 102.8 nm to 37.0 nm.

Batch Trend Plot

Peak position (left) and FWHM (right) vs. sample index for all detected reflections. Dashed lines show linear fits.

Decision Implication

This is the decision layer a process engineer needs: a same-material batch can be screened for lattice-position drift, peak broadening, and fit-quality exceptions without rebuilding the analysis in a spreadsheet. No automated review flags were raised, so the report is ready for normal scientist review. The commercial value is saved scientist time, consistent reporting, and a repeatable audit trail from raw patterns to memo.

Validation Notes

  • Scherrer crystallite sizes are upper-bound estimates; instrument broadening has not been subtracted.
  • Automated flags identify samples that need manual peak-assignment or fit-quality review before release.
  • Synthetic demo data is used here so the expected peak families and process trend are known in advance.

Anomaly Report

No anomalies detected across all samples.

Key Findings

  • Best fit: Quartz_Anneal_04 (R²=0.8537).
  • Poorest fit: Quartz_Anneal_08 (R²=0.7759). All fits acceptable.
  • Crystallite sizes range from 37.0 nm to 102.8 nm (Scherrer K=0.9, instrument broadening not subtracted).

Next Steps

  • Verify any flagged samples with manual peak assignment or reference pattern matching (PDF-4+, COD).
  • Subtract instrument broadening using a standard (LaB₆ or NIST Si SRM 640) before reporting Scherrer sizes as absolute values.
  • For multi-phase samples, consider Rietveld refinement for quantitative phase analysis.
  • Cross-reference d-spacings with published crystallographic databases for phase identification confirmation.
